articles.description: Muhoozi Kainerugaba, son of Uganda's president, made headlines by offering 100 cows for Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni's hand in marriage and demanding $1 billion from Turkey, sparking global controversy with his bold tweets and threats.

articles.title: News in Frames: Traditions in transition
articles.description: With a belief system intertwined with nature, and with music and dance intrinsic to daily life, an endangered tribe in Odisha strives to keep its visual tradition intact

articles.title: Two Supertankers U-Turn in Hormuz as US-Iran Talks Break Down
articles.description: Two empty supertankers attempted to make their way through the Strait of Hormuz and into the Persian Gulf on Sunday, only to make a last-minute U-turn just as peace negotiations between the US and Iran broke down, threatening a fragile ceasefire.

articles.description: Nelson Mandela, a key figure in ending apartheid, spent 27 years in prison for his activism.

dailyNews.title: Trump says U.S. is "clearing out" the Strait of Hormuz as U.S., Iranian officials meet in Pakistan
dailyNews.description: President Trump said the U.S. is "clearing out" the Strait of Hormuz on social media Saturday. Meanwhile, U.S. officials are meeting face-to-face with Iranian and Pakistani officials in Islamabad. CBS News' Taurean Small has the latest.

dailyNews.title: Trump policies, China's biotech boom are ending Europe's pharma powerhouse era
dailyNews.description: Companies have long lamented Europe's fragmented capital markets, single-market adoption on pricing and clinical trials, and uneven reimbursement policies.

dailyNews.title: After 16 years in power, Putin's closest friend in Europe faces a pivotal election
dailyNews.description: Few leaders have done more than Hungary’s Viktor Orbán to shape the global right-wing populism that informed President Donald Trump’s MAGA project.

dailyNews.title: Appeals court rules Trump's White House ballroom construction can temporarily move forward
dailyNews.description: A federal appeals court on Saturday temporarily extended a lower court judge's order allowing the construction of Trump's White House ballroom to move forward.

dailyNews.title: Inside Kristen Welker's interview with the Cuban president
dailyNews.description: In his first interview with a U.S. broadcast network, Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel insisted to @MeetThePress moderator Kristen Welker that he's not "stepping down," despite the Trump administration's increasing pressure on Cuba. Kristen joins "Here's the Scoop" to discuss what was happening behind the scenes during her interview.
